conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 8, and Blake, who is 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 20).

Let me ask you about each child separately to assess eligibility.

Starting with **Alex (age 8)**: Do you live with Alex in the UK, and are you responsible for their care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me, but I don't actually live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. That's important information.

Could you please tell me **which country you're currently living in?** This will help me determine if you may still be eligible for Child Benefit while living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living in Spain may affect your eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you might still be able to claim Child Benefit while living abroad.

Could you tell me: **Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces), or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Now let me ask about your older child, **Blake (age 20)**. Is Blake currently in approved education or training? (This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved vocational training)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any approved education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for Child Benefit for both of your children based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible for Child Benefit** for either of your children. Here's why:

**For Alex (age 8):**
While Alex is within the normal age range for Child Benefit (under 16), you do not meet the UK residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. Since you're living in Spain and you are not a Crown servant and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, you do not qualify for any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad.

**For Blake (age 20):**
Blake is over the age limit for Child Benefit. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training, but Blake is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, so they do not qualify.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you return to live in the UK - you may wish to reassess your eligibility at that time. If you have any questions, you can contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so they would normally qualify for Child Benefit. However, according to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The claimant is currently living in Spain. While there are exceptions for living abroad (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme), the claimant has confirmed they are not a Crown servant and do not have settled or pre-settled status. Therefore, Alex does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

Blake is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Therefore, Blake exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit (under 20 in approved education/training) and does not qualify.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (8) and Blake (20). While Alex is within the age range for Child Benefit (under 16), the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement as they are living in Spain and do not qualify for any of the exceptions that allow claiming while abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status). Blake is over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so they would normally qualify for Child Benefit. However, according to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The claimant is currently living in Spain. While there are exceptions for living abroad (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme), the claimant has confirmed they are not a Crown servant and do not have settled or pre-settled status. Therefore, Alex does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

Blake is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Therefore, Blake exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit (under 20 in approved education/training) and does not qualify.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (8) and Blake (20). While Alex is within the age range for Child Benefit (under 16), the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement as they are living in Spain and do not qualify for any of the exceptions that allow claiming while abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status). Blake is over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit as they are 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
